name: Fushimi Inari Taisha
summary: Specific site entity used as an activity and routing context for early-morning temple visits.

parent_refs.label: Kyoto
notes: Site remains attached to the city shell for transit and lodging logic.

seasonal_signals.summary: Popular shoulder-season site with crowding that changes sharply by hour.
notes: Early arrival materially improves the visit quality.

access_constraints: Stair-heavy site paths can dominate the visit once beyond the main gate.
notes: Last-mile approach is straightforward from rail.

experience_signals.label: Iconic landmark
notes: Strong fit for first-time Kyoto travelers.

adjacency_refs.summary: Pairs naturally with eastern Kyoto neighborhood exploration.

source_refs.summary: Defines the landmark as a site-level destination for itinerary attachment.
Supports early-arrival and stair-intensity assumptions for site planning.

operational_notes.summary: Expect early crowd spikes and long stair-heavy walking loops after sunrise.
notes: The first hours of the day materially change the experience quality.
